_meta.researcher: Claude Code research agent (Sony-sensor-first scope, broader-coverage replacement)
gaps: Sony IMX571 mono OSC color values: green channel inferred ~95% of mono peak, red/blue ~85% — need direct OSC absolute-QE plots to confirm.
IMX455 wavelength-specific QE is partly inferred; lab papers (Gill et al., Alarcon et al.) report Halpha 50-55% — used midpoint 50/55%, not text-quoted at every wavelength.
IMX226, IMX492 detailed wavelength values are interpolated from typical STARVIS curves rather than text-published; confidence: low.
Older Sony CCDs (ICX805) have only relative-shape data published in text form; absolute wavelength values estimated from EXview HAD II family curves.
Astrodon LRGB Gen2 E-series exact passbands are inferred from text descriptions (the 'about 60 nm wide red' claim); precise band edges not text-published.
Chroma 8 nm Halpha and 8 nm SII filters: existence confirmed but precise transmission values not text-published.
Baader UHC-S exact dual-passband edges inferred from descriptive text; full transmission curve only available as plot.
IDAS LPS D1/D2/D3 exact passband shapes are inferred from review text; precise band edges only published as plots.
Canon EOS Ra: Canon does not publish QE numbers — entries are estimates from typical Canon BSI behavior + astro-mod 4x Halpha factor.
Several mono variants (e.g. IMX294 mono) have peak QE published but per-wavelength values inferred from RGB envelope.
Player One Apollo / Ares specific QE numbers not text-published — inherit from sensor entry only.
_meta.methodology_notes: Sony IMX sensor-level data captured first; per-camera entries inherit from sensor data with confidence: medium where the chip + IR-cut stack is comparable. Wavelengths: 486 nm = Hbeta, 501 nm = OIII, 656 nm = Halpha, 672 nm = SII. Image-only sources (.jpg/.png/.webp/.pdf) were strictly AVOIDED per crash-safety rule (no Read on .jpg/.png/.webp/.pdf was performed at any point). Values come exclusively from manufacturer HTML spec text, sensor datasheet text excerpts, and reputable third-party text references (QHYCCD published measurements, ZWO product pages, Cloudy Nights threads transcribing manufacturer numbers, astrojolo.com, Atik Cameras spec text, Player One/Optolong/Baader/Antlia spec pages). Inferences for color OSC chips: when only mono peak QE is text-known, OSC channel QE estimated from typical Bayer dye-stack absorption (red/blue ~85% of mono peak, green ~95%) and noted with confidence: medium plus explicit inference notes. The four salvage entries (ASI585MC, ASI585MM, QHY268M, ASI294MC) carry their original source URLs (including the .jpg URLs) for traceability — but those images were never re-Read by this agent. Same-sensor inference is the dominant strategy: per-camera entries with qe_inherits_from_sensor: true should be looked up via sensors[<sensor>].qe.

IMX585.format: Type 1/1.2 (4/3 class) BSI CMOS, 3856x2180, 2.9um pixel, STARVIS 2
IMX585.notes: Color values from ASI585MC absolute QE plot extraction (salvaged). Mono values from QHYCCD miniCAM8M (IMX585 mono) measurements: explicit at 501.0/656.3/671.6 nm. 486 nm mono interpolated. STARVIS 2 family.

IMX571.notes: Mono values from QHY268M absolute-QE curve extraction (salvaged). QHY confirmed text-published 'OIII at least 87%, peak at least 90%'. OSC R/G/B inferred from typical Bayer dye-stack: green ~95% of mono peak, red/blue ~85%. Used in ASI2600MC/MM, QHY268C/M, Player One Poseidon, ATIK ATR3-26000, QSI 726, and others.

IMX455.notes: Same 3.76um pixel architecture as IMX571, scaled to 35mm full frame. Mono peak QHY-published 90%; ZWO ASI6200MM published 91%. Halpha QE measured at ~50-55% via lab work (Gill et al., Alarcon et al., Cloudy Nights). OSC R/G/B inferred via Bayer stack typical attenuation.

IMX410.notes: Used in QHY410C, ASI2400MC, Nikon Z6, Sony A7 III. Peak QE 80% (back-illuminated). No mono variant exists. OSC values inferred from typical Bayer stack against 80% peak.

IMX294.notes: Color values from ASI294MC plot extraction (salvaged). Mono values for IMX294MM-equivalent inferred — IMX294 in mono variant adds ~5-10% over color green channel. 4-corner color filter pattern (close to RGGB).

IMX464.format: Type 1/1.8 BSI CMOS, 2712x1538, 2.9um pixel, 4.2 MP, STARVIS
IMX464.notes: Player One reports peak QE ~91%. Higher NIR sensitivity than IMX462 (deeper photodiode wells).

IMX462.format: Type 1/2.8 BSI CMOS, 1944x1096, 2.9um pixel, 2.1 MP, STARVIS
IMX462.notes: Sixth-gen Sony Exmor R with deeper pixels. Excellent NIR response (~50% at 850nm). Peak QE typically 80-87% depending on source. Used in ASI462MC, QHY5III462C, Player One Mars-C/Mars-M-II.

IMX178.format: Type 1/1.8 BSI CMOS, 3096x2080, 2.4um pixel, 6.4 MP, STARVIS
IMX178.notes: Peak QE typically reported 81% mono. Color version Bayer-attenuated. Older STARVIS generation.

IMX183.notes: ZWO product page: peak QE 84%, Halpha QE >60% (matches 62% estimate). Color and mono use same chip, color attenuated by Bayer.

IMX174.format: Type 1/1.2 global-shutter CMOS, 1936x1216, 5.86um pixel, 2.3 MP, Pregius Gen1
IMX174.notes: Pregius global-shutter CMOS. Peak QE 77-78% mono. Excellent for guiding/lucky-imaging. Wavelength QE estimates from typical Pregius spectral response shape.

IMX533.notes: ZWO published: ASI533MM peak QE >91%, ASI533MC peak QE >80%. Same fab as IMX571 family. Square 1-inch sensor. Used in ASI533MC/MM, Player One Saturn-C.

IMX676.format: Type 1/1.6 (square) BSI CMOS, 3552x3552, 2.0um pixel, 12.6 MP, STARVIS 2
IMX676.notes: STARVIS 2. ZWO measured peak QE 83% mono. Square 1/1.6" form factor. Used in ASI676MM/MC.

IMX678.format: Type 1/1.8 BSI CMOS, 3840x2160, 2.0um pixel, 8.29 MP, STARVIS 2
IMX678.notes: STARVIS 2 family with IMX676. Peak QE 83%. Used in ASI678MC/MM, Player One Apollo-C/M.

IMX485.format: Type 1/1.2 BSI CMOS, 3840x2160, 2.9um pixel, 8.29 MP, STARVIS
IMX485.notes: ZWO published peak QE 85% at 530nm. STARVIS first generation. Color-only (no mono variant produced for astro).

IMX264.format: Type 2/3 global-shutter CMOS, 2448x2048, 3.45um pixel, 5.0 MP, Pregius Gen2
IMX264.notes: Sony Pregius Gen2 global-shutter. Peak QE ~70%. Used in QHY264, ASI264MM, machine-vision applications.

IMX226.notes: Older 1.85um pixel STARVIS. Often confused with IMX224. Peak QE estimates 75-80%.

IMX224.format: Type 1/3 BSI CMOS, 1304x976, 3.75um pixel, 1.27 MP, STARVIS
IMX224.notes: ZWO ASI224MC reported peak QE 80%. Original STARVIS. Excellent NIR response for planetary.

IMX492.notes: Used in QHY294C/M (Pro). Smaller-pixel relative of IMX294. QHY uses pixel-binning to expose either at full 47MP@2.315um or binned 11.7MP@4.63um. Peak QE estimated 70-75%.

KAF-8300.notes: Kodak datasheet (now ON Semi): peak QE 56% at 540nm with microlens + AR glass. Used in Atik 383L+, QSI 583, Moravian G3-8300.

KAF-16200.notes: Kodak datasheet: peak QE 56% at 540nm. Used in Atik 16200, Moravian G3-16200, FLI Microline ML16200.

ICX694.format: Type 1 EXview HAD II CCD, 2750x2200, 4.54um pixel, 6.0 MP
ICX694.notes: EXview HAD II with very flat broadband response. Mono peak ~77%, OIII ~67%, Halpha ~65%. Used in Atik 460EX, QHY22, QSI660, Moravian G3.

ICX805.format: Type 1 EXview HAD II CCD, 3380x2704, 3.69um pixel, 9.1 MP
ICX805.notes: EXview HAD II. Used in QSI 690, Atik 490EX. QE estimates inferred from EXview HAD II family curves; precise numerical wavelength values not text-published.

asi585mc.notes: Salvaged from prior agent's ZWO absolute-QE plot extraction. Peak QE 91%.

asi585mm.notes: Inherits IMX585 mono QE from sensor entry.

qhy268m.notes: Salvaged QHY269M curve extraction; QHY explicitly published 'OIII at least 87%, peak at least 90%'.

asi294mc.notes: Salvaged plot extraction; peak QE 75% applied. 4-corner color filter pattern treated as RGGB.

asi294mm.notes: Inherits IMX294 mono envelope from sensor entry. ZWO published peak QE ~75-78% mono.

asi2600mc.notes: Inherits IMX571 OSC. ZWO published peak QE ~80-91% (color version typically ~80%).

asi2600mm.notes: Inherits IMX571 mono. ZWO peak QE 91%.

qhy268c.notes: Inherits IMX571 OSC. Same chip as QHY268M and ASI2600MC.

asi6200mc.notes: Inherits IMX455 OSC. ZWO published peak QE 80% color version.

asi6200mm.notes: Inherits IMX455 mono. ZWO published peak QE 91%.

qhy600m.notes: Inherits IMX455 mono. QHY published peak QE 90%.

asi2400mc.notes: Inherits IMX410 OSC. Peak QE 80%. Full-frame, 5.94um pixel.

qhy128c.notes: Same IMX410 chip as ASI2400MC, QHY410C. (QHY128C uses IMX410; QHY128A uses IMX128.)

qhy410c.notes: Inherits IMX410. QHY published peak QE 80% with AR+AR coated optical window.

qhy294c.notes: Inherits IMX294 OSC. (QHY294C-Pro switches to IMX492 in unbinned mode.)

qhy294m.notes: Inherits IMX492 mono. Pro version supports binned (4.63um) or unbinned (2.315um) modes.

asi462mc.notes: Inherits IMX462 OSC. Excellent NIR response (~50% at 850nm).

asi224mc.notes: Inherits IMX224. ZWO published peak QE 80%.

asi183mm.notes: Inherits IMX183 mono. Peak QE 84%, Halpha >60%.

asi174mm.notes: Inherits IMX174. Global shutter, peak QE 78%.

asi533mc.notes: Inherits IMX533 OSC. ZWO peak QE >80%. Square format.

asi676mm.notes: Inherits IMX676 mono. ZWO measured peak QE 83%.

asi678mc.notes: Inherits IMX678 OSC. STARVIS 2, peak QE 83%.

asi485mc.notes: Inherits IMX485. ZWO published peak QE 85% at 530 nm.

atik-460ex.notes: Inherits ICX694 mono. EXview HAD II CCD.

atik-16200.notes: Inherits KAF-16200. Kodak/ON Semi datasheet peak QE 56%.

atik-383l.notes: Inherits KAF-8300 mono. Discontinued but still common in used market.

asi1600mm.notes: Panasonic MN34230 4/3 sensor. ZWO published peak QE ~60%. Older non-Sony sensor; broadly displaced by IMX571-based ASI2600MM.

asi071mc.notes: Sony IMX071 APS-C. ZWO published peak QE ~50%. Older sensor; predecessor to IMX571 family.

qhy461m.notes: Sony IMX461 medium-format BSI. 100MP. Peak QE ~90%.

canon-eos-ra.notes: 30.3 MP full-frame, 5.36um. IR-cut filter modified for 4x Halpha transmission @ 656 nm vs stock EOS R. Canon does not publish QE; estimates inferred from typical Canon BSI dark-frame analysis.

nikon-z6-ii.notes: Z6 II uses Sony IMX410 (same as A7 III, ASI2400MC, QHY410C). Stock filter blocks Halpha; astro-modified versions widely available.

sony-a7-iii.notes: Sony A7 III uses IMX410. Same chip as ASI2400MC, QHY410C, Nikon Z6.

Astrodon-Ha-3nm-50mm.notes: Astrodon guarantees >90% peak (typical 92-97%). 3 nm helps separate Halpha from NII 658.4 nm. Narrowband premium filter.

Astrodon-Ha-5nm-50mm.notes: 5 nm Astrodon series. Peak transmittance >90% (typical 95-98%) at emission line. Best for f/5 and slower.

Astrodon-OIII-3nm-50mm.notes: Centered on OIII 500.7 nm. Astrodon guarantees >90% peak (typical 92-97%).

Astrodon-NII-3nm-50mm.notes: Astrodon NII line filter at 658.4 nm. Specialized for NII vs Halpha differentiation.

Astrodon-LRGB-Gen2-E-Series-Lum.notes: Luminance: NIR/UV-blocked colorless. ~400-700 nm passband. 3 mm fused silica substrate. OD5 OOB blocking.

Astrodon-LRGB-Gen2-E-Series-R.notes: Astrodon E-series red is ~60 nm wide deep red, blocks high-pressure sodium and includes Halpha + SII + NII.

Astrodon-LRGB-Gen2-E-Series-G.notes: Green ~495-575 nm. Includes OIII line for emission-nebula contrast.

Astrodon-LRGB-Gen2-E-Series-B.notes: Blue ~400-500 nm. Includes Hbeta. Tru-Balance equalizes red flux for Kodak E-series CCDs.

Chroma-Ha-3nm.notes: Premium Chroma 3 nm Halpha. Outstanding transmittance 99.12% at 656.3 nm. Optional CWL pre-shifted version for f/3 systems.

Chroma-Ha-5nm.notes: Chroma 5 nm Halpha. For faster optics where 3 nm CWL shift is problematic.

Chroma-Ha-8nm.notes: Chroma 8 nm Halpha. Wide variant for very fast optics.

Chroma-Ha-3nm-F3-Highspeed.notes: CWL pre-shifted into red so f/3 cone-induced blueshift centers it on 656.3 nm. For RASA, Hyperstar, fast astrographs.

Astronomik-Ha-6nm-CCD.notes: Astronomik 6 nm CCD Halpha. Guaranteed >90%, typical 93%. Special MFR coating for fast optics down to f/4.

Astronomik-Ha-12nm-CCD.notes: Astronomik 12 nm Halpha. Typical 96%. Compatible with f/3 fast systems.

Astronomik-OIII-6nm-CCD.notes: Astronomik 6 nm CCD OIII. Includes 496 nm and 500.7 nm doublet. Max transmission ~510 nm. Typical 92%.

Astronomik-OIII-12nm-CCD.notes: Astronomik 12 nm OIII. Wider variant for fast optics, MFR coating.

Astronomik-DeepSky-RGB-Type2c-R.notes: Type 2c sRGB-aligned. Red includes Halpha and SII. Special coatings for fast optics.

Astronomik-DeepSky-RGB-Type2c-G.notes: Type 2c green. Includes OIII at edge.

Astronomik-L1-UV-IR-Block.notes: L-1 wider passband (~380-700 nm) for visual/imaging UV+IR cut.

Astronomik-L2-UV-IR-Block.notes: L-2 narrower for sharper-cutoff than L-1.

Astronomik-L3-UV-IR-Block.notes: L-3 narrowest, cuts UV-induced bloating in fast refractors.

Optolong-Lpro.notes: Multi-bandpass LPR. ~90% at OIII (496/500.7), Hbeta (486), NII (654/658), Halpha (656), SII (672). Notches at Na 589, Hg 435/578.

Optolong-LeNhance.notes: Tri-band: Halpha+NII (24nm wide), OIII (10nm), Hbeta (10nm). For OSC cameras, urban skies.

Optolong-LeXtreme-7nm.notes: Dual 7 nm Halpha + OIII. ~90% transmission. Designed for OSC cameras under heavy LP. Halpha and OIII isolated for SHO synthesis from a single OSC frame.

Optolong-LUltimate-3nm.notes: Dual 3 nm Halpha + OIII for OSC. Narrower than L-eXtreme; more aggressive LPR.

Optolong-LeXtreme-F2.notes: F/2 high-speed L-eXtreme. CWL pre-shifted to red for f/2 cone.

Optolong-LRGB-L.notes: Optolong L: 380-700 nm, blocks 700-1100 nm. B270 substrate, 1.85 mm.

Optolong-LRGB-R.notes: Optolong R: includes Halpha, NII, SII. Tave >90%.

Optolong-LRGB-G.notes: Optolong G: gap to R near 580 nm blocks Na 589.

Optolong-UV-IR-Cut.notes: Standard UV/IR cut. Common front-mount filter for OSC cameras lacking integrated cut.

Baader-Ha-3.5nm-f2-CMOS.notes: Baader f/2 Ultra-Highspeed 3.5 nm Halpha CMOS-optimized. CWL pre-shifted for f/3.4 to f/1.8 systems. Life-Coat hard coating.

Baader-OIII-4nm-f2-CMOS.notes: Baader f/2 Ultra-Highspeed 4 nm OIII CMOS-optimized.

Baader-SII-4nm-f2-CMOS.notes: Baader f/2 Ultra-Highspeed 4 nm SII CMOS-optimized.

Baader-Ha-6.5nm-CMOS.notes: Baader 6.5 nm CMOS Halpha. For f/10 to f/3.5 systems. Reflex-Blocker hard coat.

Baader-UHC-S.notes: Baader UHC-S: ~60 nm passband around OIII+Hbeta and a separate ~25 nm window around Halpha. >97% peak, >99% Halpha.

Antlia-Ha-3nm-Pro.notes: Antlia 3 nm Pro Halpha. T>88% within 1 nm of CWL.

Antlia-Ha-4.5nm-Edge.notes: Antlia EDGE 4.5 nm Halpha. Mid-bandwidth alternative.

Antlia-ALP-T-Ha-OIII-5nm.notes: Antlia ALP-T 5 nm dual: Halpha 90% / OIII 82%. Premium OSC dual narrowband.

Antlia-ALP-T-Ha-OIII-3nm.notes: Antlia ALP-T 3 nm dual Halpha + OIII.

Antlia-ALP-T-SII-OIII-3nm.notes: Antlia ALP-T 3 nm dual: SII + OIII (the SHO complement to Halpha-OIII for full SHO with two OSC frames).

Antlia-ALP-T-SII-Hb-5nm.notes: Antlia ALP-T 5 nm dual SII + Hbeta. Pairs with Halpha-OIII ALP-T to extract all four narrowband lines from OSC.

ZWO-Ha-7nm.notes: ZWO 7 nm Halpha. Min 80%, typical 90%. FWHM 7 ± 0.5 nm.

ZWO-LRGB-L.notes: ZWO L: similar to other inexpensive LRGB sets.

IDAS-LPS-D1.notes: IDAS Light Pollution Suppression D1 — older non-LED-optimized version. Passes Hbeta, OIII, Halpha, C2 Swan bands. Bandwidth 400-700 nm with notches.

IDAS-LPS-D2.notes: IDAS LPS D2 — adds LED-light notches over D1.

IDAS-LPS-D3.notes: IDAS LPS D3 — current version, more triband-like, broader Halpha pass.

IDAS-NBZ.notes: IDAS NBZ — 10 nm Halpha + 10 nm OIII for OSC. Halo-suppressed redesign of NBX. Wider than L-eXtreme.

SVBony-SV220-7nm.notes: SVBony SV220 7 nm dual Halpha + OIII. T>94% Halpha, T>90% OIII. Budget OSC dual-NB option.

SVBony-SV220-7nm-SII-OIII.notes: SVBony SV220 7 nm dual SII + OIII. Pairs with Halpha-OIII for SHO from OSC.

SVBony-SV220-3nm.notes: SVBony SV220 3 nm dual narrowband. Tighter than 7 nm version.

Optolong-L-Synergy-7nm.notes: Optolong L-Synergy: dual 7 nm SII + OIII. Pairs with L-eXtreme to obtain SHO from OSC.

Optolong-L-Quad-Enhance.notes: L-Quad Enhance Filter (L-QEF): four bands - Hbeta + OIII + Halpha + SII. >90% at the four nebula lines. Bortle 1-7 recommended. Halpha bandwidth wider to include NII contribution.

STC-Astro-Duo-NB.notes: STC Optical Astro Duo-NB: 10 nm Halpha + 10 nm OIII. Transmission >85%. IBAD coating.

STC-Astro-Multispectra.notes: STC Multispectra LPR. Broad passband with multi-notch LP suppression. Detailed spec text limited.

Askar-ColorMagic-D1-Ha-OIII.notes: Askar Color Magic D1: Halpha 8.5 nm (wider to capture NII 658.4) + OIII 6.5 nm. Peak transmission 85%.

Askar-ColorMagic-D2-SII-OIII.notes: Askar Color Magic D2: SII 8.5 nm + OIII 6.5 nm. Pairs with D1 to extract full SHO from OSC.

Astronomik-CLS.notes: Astronomik CLS (City Light Suppression). ~99 nm wide LPR with multi-notch. Compromise between broadband fidelity and LPR.

Astronomik-UHC.notes: Astronomik UHC: passes Hbeta, OIII (496+500.7), and Halpha. Blocks Na/Hg vapor and natural airglow lines. Visual + photographic.
